purpose: Wrap tmux to drive a long-running Claude Code (CC) TUI session: spawn detached panes, send keystrokes, capture pane text, and parse sentinel-prefixed output lines into cc_output events.

public_surface.factory: CcTmuxAdapter.factory(actor_id, config) -> CcTmuxAdapter (pure, no I/O — PRD 04 F02)
public_surface.dispatch: async on_directive(action, args) -> {'ok': bool, 'result'?: dict, 'error'?: str}
public_surface.event_stream: async emit_events() generator yielding cc_output event dicts

rules: line must start with the exact prefix (no leading whitespace/ANSI)
trailing newline stripped, internal whitespace preserved verbatim
bare '[esr-cc]' with no payload is treated as noise (returns None)
non-sentinel lines return None so the poller skips cheaply

emit_events_behavior.session_name_source: actor_id after ':' split — actor_id is 'tmux:<thread_id>' so session_name == <thread_id>
emit_events_behavior.dedup: set of already-seen full lines (by string)
emit_events_behavior.startup_race_tolerance: up to 1200 consecutive misses (~10 min) before giving up, to cover the race where emit_events starts before init_directive creates the session
emit_events_behavior.exit_condition: tmux probe fails OR missing_limit reached

env_passing.mechanism: new_session args.env is a dict[str, str]; adapter expands each pair into ['-e', 'K=V'] argv entries before start_cmd
env_passing.cwd_mechanism: new_session args.cwd (str) is forwarded as tmux '-c <cwd>' flag
env_passing.session_name_received_via: args.session_name on new_session; for emit_events it is derived from actor_id ('tmux:<thread_id>')
env_passing.legacy_compat: when env/cwd are absent, argv contains neither -e nor -c — clean pass-through

capability_scan.expected: no violations against allowed_io (only 'tmux' subprocess calls present)

factory_purity.guarantee: factory never calls subprocess.run/Popen; tmux probe deferred until first on_directive
factory_purity.verified_by: tests/test_factory_purity.py (monkeypatches both subprocess.run and subprocess.Popen to raise)

files.test_registration.py: 3 tests — ADAPTER_REGISTRY entry, allowed_io, factory instance
files.test_env.py: 2 tests — missing-tmux graceful error + probe caching (1 probe for N directives)
files.test_directives.py: 10 tests — new_session argv/error/unknown, send_keys verbatim content, kill_session, capture_pane, v0.2 env + cwd + legacy argv
files.test_events.py: 5 tests — sentinel match, non-sentinel ignored, trailing newline stripped, internal whitespace preserved, adapter.parse_line alias exposed

key_design_notes: Factory is pure (F02) — adapter can be instantiated without tmux installed; probe runs lazily on first on_directive and caches its bool result.
Sentinel scheme isolates ESR event signal from arbitrary terminal output/ANSI noise; payload after '[esr-cc] ' is passed verbatim so downstream handlers can carry JSON or free text.
send_keys uses argv form (list) — no shell interpretation, so $vars, backticks, and embedded quotes reach tmux as literal keystrokes.
emit_events tolerates a long startup race (~10 min) because the async generator is scheduled on channel-join while init_directive runs separately.
risk_notes: Line-based dedup in emit_events keeps an unbounded set of seen lines for the session's lifetime — could grow for extremely long-running CCs.
_ensure_tmux() does not check the completed process returncode — any tmux binary that exits non-zero on --version would still be treated as available; only FileNotFoundError flips the cache to False.
